| San Francisco | San Francisco | Yes |
| Seattle | Seattle | Yes |
| Ventura / Thousand Oaks | Ventura / Thousand Oaks | Yes |
| Orange County / Long Beach | Los Angeles — Long Beach | Partial — "Long Beach" appears in both but labels differ |

**Action item for Sprint 1:** Build a metro region normalization dictionary:
```python
REGION_GROUPS = {
    "Los Angeles": ["Los Angeles", "Los Angeles — West", "Los Angeles — North",
                    "Los Angeles — East", "Los Angeles — Long Beach"],
    "Orange County / Long Beach": ["Los Angeles — Long Beach"],
}
```

## 7. Data Gaps and Risks

- [ ] **No speaker availability/scheduling data** — `calendar_fit` scoring will use a flat 0.5 baseline for all speakers until availability data is collected. Mitigation: Add a "preferred regions" field to speaker profiles in Sprint 1 UI.
- [ ] **No historical engagement data** — `historical_conversion` scoring will use flat 0.5 baseline for all speakers. Mitigation: Sprint 2 pipeline tracker will begin accumulating conversion data.
- [ ] **Events CSV has sub-entries** (same event, different contacts) — 3 "OUR" rows and 2 "Bronco Startup Challenge" rows. Dedup by event name in Sprint 1 matching engine.
- [ ] **Parenthetical company names** — Sean McKenna `(prev. Luth, Toluna)`, Calvin Friesth `(consultative client development)`, Adam Portner `(prev. RealityMine, ...)`. These produce slightly noisy embedding text. Mitigation: Strip parenthetical content in `compose_speaker_text()` in Sprint 1 if validation shows degraded match quality.
- [ ] **Course Guest Lecture Fit is subjective** — No documented criteria for High/Medium/Low assignments. All 10 "High" courses are research/analytics-focused, which aligns well with board expertise but may create a scoring bias toward research events over career fairs.
- [ ] **Orange County / Long Beach region gap** — Only Rob Kaiser is geographically close, and his sub-region label ("Los Angeles — Long Beach") won't fuzzy-match "Orange County / Long Beach" without the normalization dictionary.
- [ ] **Seattle and Portland single-speaker risk** — If Greg Carter (Seattle) or Katie Nelson (Portland) is unavailable, those calendar events have no local speaker candidates. Mitigation: Allow cross-region matching with distance penalty in `geo_proximity` scoring.
